items.briefing:
Scamwatch is warning Australians about a rise in scams impersonating the Australian Taxation Office (ATO) and myGov. These scams typically pressure people to act quickly — claiming unpaid tax debts or account issues — to steal personal or financial information. Separately, a new Dark Reading report highlights that cybercriminals are increasingly targeting education technology *suppliers* rather than schools directly, which is relevant for RTOs using third-party learning platforms or student management systems.

Australia's communications regulator, ACMA, has also outlined its 2026–27 consumer protection priorities, signalling increased scrutiny on organisations handling personal data. **Tip:** If you receive an unexpected ATO or myGov message, don't click any links — log in directly through the official website to verify.

featured.title: Australia's resilience problem isn't redundancy. It's shared infrastructure
featured.quote: Australia's critical infrastructure vulnerability through shared fibre routes poses systemic resilience risks.
